ORDER DENYING PLAINTIFF'S MOTIONS TO COMPEL FURTHER DISCOVERY

LAW AND MOTION, 302, RULING - NOTICE TO CONTEST THE TENTATIVE RULING WAS NOT TIMELY GIVEN. THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING. PLAINTIFF ERIN COLLINS' MOTION TO COMPEL DEFENDANT TO PROVIDE FURTHER RESPONSES TO PLAINTIFF'S FORM INTERROGATORY- GENERAL (SET ONE) IS DENIED. GIVEN THE ORDER GRANTING PRELIMINARY APPROVAL OF CLASS ACTION SETTLEMENT ISSUED ON MARCH 24, 2014 IN HARMON V. THE GAP, INC. (RIVERSIDE CTY. SUP. CT., CASE NO. RIC 120620) ("HARMON"), AT THIS JUNCTURE, DEFENDANTS ARE NOT REQUIRED TO PROVIDE FURTHER RESPONSES UNTIL THE CONCLUSION OF THAT SETTLEMENT. IT IS CLEAR FROM THE PLEADINGS IN BOTH CASES THAT PLAINTIFF IN THIS ACTION IS WITHIN THE CLASS COVERED BY THE PLEADINGS IN HARMON AND THAT THE CLASS THAT PLAINTIFF SEEKS TO REPRESENT IN THIS CASE AS WELL AS THE ALLEGED WRONGS SOUGHT TO BE REMEDIED ARE ESSENTIALLY THE SAME WRONGS AS ASSERTED IN HARMON. IT WOULD BE A WASTE OF THE PARTIES' RESOURCES AND OF THE COURT'S JUDICIAL EFFORTS TO REQUIRE FURTHER DISCOVERY AT THIS POINT. WHEN THE SETTLEMENT IN HARMON IS FINALIZED, PLAINTIFF IN THIS ACTION MAY EITHER ACCEPT THE TERMS OF THE SETTLEMENT OR OPT OUT OF ANY SETTLEMENT AND PROCEED IN THIS ACTION SEEKING TO REPRESENT HERSELF AND/OR TO REPRESENT ALL OTHER ALLEGED CLASS MEMBERS WHO MAY SIMILARLY OPT OUT OF THE HARMON SETTLEMENT. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: ERNEST H. GOLDSMITH, CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT ; COURT REPORTER: TAMRA ELAINE KEEN CSR # 5404

WHEN THE SETTLEMENT IN HARMON IS FINALIZED, PLAINTIFF IN THIS ACTION MAY EITHER ACCEPT THE TERMS OF THE SETTLEMENT OR OPT OUT OF ANY SETTLEMENT AND PROCEED IN THIS ACTION SEEKING TO REPRESENT HERSELF AND/OR TO REPRESENT ALL OTHER ALLEGED CLASS MEMBERS WHO MAY SIMILARLY OPT OUT OF THE HARMON SETTLEMENT. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: ERNEST H. GOLDSMITH, CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT ; COURT REPORTER: TAMRA ELAINE KEEN CSR # 5404

Notice Of Moiton And Motion To Compel Deft To Provide Further Responses To Pltf'S Requests For Production Of Documents (Set One)
Off Calendar Apr-10-2014 Denied
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. EDWIN AIWAZIAN APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFF BY TELEPHONE APPEARANCE;
NO OTHER APPEARANCES ON THE MATTER;
TAMRA ELAINE KEEN, TEL. NO. 591-3333, COURT REPORTER.

RULING - NOTICE TO CONTEST THE TENTATIVE RULING WAS NOT TIMELY GIVEN. The Court adopted its tentative ruling.PLAINTIFF ERIN COLLINS' Motion To Compel Defendant To Provide Further Responses To Plaintiff's Requests For Production Of Documents (Set One) is Denied. Given the Order Granting Preliminary Approval of Class Action Settlement issued on March 24, 2014 in Harmon v. The Gap, Inc. (Riverside Cty. Sup. Ct., case no. RIC 120620) ("Harmon"), at this juncture, Defendants are not required to provide further responses until the conclusion of that settlement. It is clear from the pleadings in both cases that Plaintiff in this action is within the class covered by the pleadings in Harmon and that the class that Plaintiff seeks to represent in this case as well as the alleged wrongs sought to be remedied are essentially the same wrongs as asserted in Harmon. It would be a waste of the parties' resources and of the court's judicial efforts to require further discovery at this point. When the settlement in Harmon is finalized, Plaintiff in this action may either accept the terms of the settlement or opt out of any settlement and proceed in this action seeking to represent herself and/or to represent all other alleged class members who may similarly opt out of the Harmon settlement. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith, Clerk: Cynthia Herbert ; Court Reporter: Tamra Elaine Keen CSR # 5404
